SEARCH

Class PriorityQueueTest

java.lang.Object
PriorityQueueTest
public class PriorityQueueTest extends Object
Unit tests for WeightedElement, MyPriorityQueue and MyClassicPriorityQueue. Each MyPriorityQueue test uses a java.util.PriorityQueue as an oracle to verify that poll order and peek behavior match the standard library. Tests are grouped by class and cover edge cases as well as general cases using WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'Integer,Integer> and WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'String,String>.
Version:
4/28
Author:
Ali Kablanbek

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    PriorityQueueTest()
     

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    testAddReturnValueAndIsHeap()
    add must return true and the heap property must hold after every insertion.
    void
    testClassicDecreaseKeyWeightedIntInt()
    decreaseKey should work correctly for WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'Integer,Integer>.
    void
    testClassicDecreaseKeyWeightedStringStringRejected()
    decreaseKey on WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'String,String> with a larger weight must return null.
    void
    testClassicPQEmptyBehavior()
    An empty classic queue should return null and have an empty map.
    void
    testClassicPQMapIndicesConsistentAfterAdd()
    Every element in the heap should have its index correctly recorded in the map.
    void
    testClassicPQMapIndicesConsistentAfterPoll()
    The map should remain consistent after a poll operation
    void
    testClassicPQMatchesJavaPQ()
    Poll order of MyClassicPriorityQueue must match java.util.PriorityQueue.
    void
    testClassicPQPollOrder()
    poll should return elements in ascending order.
    void
    testClassicPQSingleElement()
    A single-element classic queue should add the element to the map at index 0.
    void
    testClassicWeightedIntIntMatchesJavaPQ()
    Poll order for WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'Integer,Integer> in classic queue must match oracle.
    void
    testClassicWeightedStringStringMatchesJavaPQ()
    Poll order for WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'String,String> in classic queue must match oracle.
    void
    testDecreaseKeyEqualValueReturnsNull()
    decreaseKey should return null when smallerValue equals target.
    void
    testDecreaseKeyLargerValueReturnsNull()
    decreaseKey should return null without any change when smallerValue is larger.
    void
    testDecreaseKeyLeafElement()
    decreaseKey on a leaf element should restore heap property and update poll order.
    void
    testDecreaseKeyMapUpdatedCorrectly()
    After decreaseKey the old key must be removed from the map and the new key added.
    void
    testDecreaseKeyMiddleElement()
    decreaseKey on a mid-heap element should bubble it up correctly.
    void
    testDecreaseKeyNotInQueueReturnsNull()
    decreaseKey should return null when target is not present in the queue.
    void
    testDecreaseKeyRootElement()
    decreaseKey on the current root element should still return the correct old value.
    void
    testDuplicateValues()
    Duplicate values must be handled correctly and match the oracle.
    void
    testEmptyQueueBehavior()
    An empty queue should return null for peek and poll and report isHeap true.
    void
    testMatchesJavaPQIntegers()
    poll order and peek must match java.util.PriorityQueue for a general Integer sequence.
    void
    testPeekDoesNotRemove()
    peek should not change the size or the next poll result.
    void
    testSingleElement()
    A queue with one element should behave correctly for all operations.
    void
    testWeightedElementCompareToIntInt()
    compareTo for Integer weight: ordering should match Integer.compareTo.
    void
    testWeightedElementCompareToStringString()
    compareTo for String weight: ordering should match String.compareTo.
    void
    testWeightedElementEquals()
    equals should be based on both element and weight fields.
    void
    testWeightedElementGetters()
    Getters should return the exact values passed to the constructor.
    void
    testWeightedElementHashCode()
    hashCode must be consistent with equals for HashMap usage.
    void
    testWeightedIntIntEqualWeights()
    Equal weights with different elements: all elements should still drain correctly.
    void
    testWeightedIntIntMatchesJavaPQ()
    Poll order by weight must match the oracle for WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'Integer,Integer>.
    void
    testWeightedStringStringMatchesJavaPQ()
    Poll order by String weight must match the oracle for WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'String,String>.
    void
    testWeightedStringStringSingleElement()
    Single-element WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'String,String> edge case.

    clone, equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

      equals should be based on both element and weight fields.

      public void testWeightedElementHashCode()
      hashCode must be consistent with equals for HashMap usage.

    • testEmptyQueueBehavior

      public void testEmptyQueueBehavior()
      An empty queue should return null for peek and poll and report isHeap true.

      public void testSingleElement()
      A queue with one element should behave correctly for all operations.

      public void testPeekDoesNotRemove()
      peek should not change the size or the next poll result.

      add must return true and the heap property must hold after every insertion.

      poll order and peek must match java.util.PriorityQueue for a general Integer sequence.

      Duplicate values must be handled correctly and match the oracle.

      public void testWeightedIntIntMatchesJavaPQ()
      Poll order by weight must match the oracle for WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'Integer,Integer>.

      public void testWeightedIntIntEqualWeights()
      Equal weights with different elements: all elements should still drain correctly.

      public void testWeightedStringStringMatchesJavaPQ()
      Poll order by String weight must match the oracle for WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'String,String>.

      An empty classic queue should return null and have an empty map.

      A single-element classic queue should add the element to the map at index 0.

      poll should return elements in ascending order.

      Every element in the heap should have its index correctly recorded in the map.

      decreaseKey on a leaf element should restore heap property and update poll order.

      decreaseKey on the current root element should still return the correct old value.
